mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0%, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >55% of observations where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and capital demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal yields positive 20d returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During severe economic contractions, extreme negative deviations in industrial production trigger short-covering rallies and policy intervention expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO troughs are negative in >70% of instances, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter increases in real GDP reflect expanding economic output and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for GDPC1 growth against 60d forward returns falls below 0.01, or positive GDP growth coincides with negative equity returns in >50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 215.0, inflation expectations rise and force central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 215.0, or the threshold effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in total nonfarm payrolls and increases in the unemployment rate confirm a broad-based labor market deterioration, reducing consumer confidence and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor weakness signal produces positive 60d returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC increases) relative to single-proxy baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Stabilization or month-over-month increases in housing starts signal the bottoming of the real estate cycle and anticipate broader economic recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing start improvements are followed by negative 60d equity returns in >60% of cases, or the signal reverses direction when RSAFS declines by more than 2.0% month-over-month.
